exact.secant_average: S_(a,b)=(v_b-v_a)/(u_b-u_a) is the weighted average of sigma_a,...,sigma_(b-1) with weights u_j-u_(j+1)>0

proof_boundary: This artifact proves the required arbitrary-column reshaped-Hankel signs for orders two and three at lambda=0 by exact planar secant geometry. It does not prove order four or higher, the all-order sign-regular-to-Jensen transfer, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

rows.claim: Positive column scaling converts every three-row Hankel column into one planar point.
Strict coefficient log-concavity orders the planar abscissas.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses the strict upper ratio wall for the actual Xi trajectory.

rows.claim: A contiguous determinant is exactly the product of two abscissa gaps and one edge-slope difference.
The completed contiguous order-three theorem makes all successive edge slopes strictly decreasing.
rows.proof_boundary: Uses the all-shift contiguous theorem, not finite evidence.

rows.claim: Every longer secant slope is a positive weighted average of its edge slopes.
rows.formula: S_(a,b)=(v_b-v_a)/(u_b-u_a) is the weighted average of sigma_a,...,sigma_(b-1) with weights u_j-u_(j+1)>0

rows.claim: All arbitrary-column three-row reshaped-Hankel determinants have the required negative sign.
rows.proof_boundary: Complete order three for the consecutive-row reshaped-Hankel target.

rows.claim: The same strict ratio order gives the arbitrary-column order-two sign.
The planar secant argument has no automatic order-four analogue; compound order four and the sign-regular-to-Jensen transfer remain open.
rows.formula: orders 1,2,3 closed; order 4 and higher open
rows.proof_boundary: Not an all-order theorem, PF-infinity, RH, or Lambda<=0.

status: exact all-column reshaped-Hankel order-two and order-three theorem at lambda=0 with order four open
